description_long: This object consists of two wooden paddles crossed at their handles, each featuring a long, straight shaft and a broad, flat blade. The paddles are crafted from a medium-brown wood, with a smooth, matte finish that highlights the natural grain. The handles are straight and squared at the ends, providing a simple grip, while the blades are wide and slightly tapered, with a rectangular profile and gently rounded corners. There are no visible markings, logos, or decorative elements on the paddles. The construction appears solid and functional, suitable for rowing or display. The overall appearance is clean and well-maintained, with no visible signs of wear or damage.
description: A pair of crossed wooden paddles with straight handles and broad, rectangular blades, finished in a smooth, medium-brown wood.
description_view_0: Front view showing both paddles crossed symmetrically, highlighting the broad, flat blades and straight handles.
description_view_1: Angled side view emphasizing the thickness of the handles and the flatness of the blades, with one blade partially overlapping the other.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the squared ends of the handles and the full width of the blades, with the crossing point clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, similar to view 1 but with the other paddle blade in the foreground, showing the same handle and blade features.
attribute_blade_design: rectangular blade with gently rounded corners, flat profile, no visible patterns or reinforcements
